introSection: Sidewinder is a steel launched shuttle roller coaster located at Elitch Gardens in Denver, Colorado, USA. Built by Arrow Development, the ride opened in 1980 at Magic Springs. It is one of three Launched Loop installations still operating today.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride debuted in 1980 as Roaring Tornado at Magic Springs in Arkansas. It closed in 1989 and was sent to Elitch Gardens, where it reopened in 1990 as Sidewinder. Sidewinder was dismantled and rebuilt when Elitch Gardens moved to a new location for the 1995 season.

After standing but not operating since 2022, Sidewinder reopened on June 28, 2025. The ride's drive motor and control system were updated during its closure.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: The train is launched down a hill and into a vertical loop. It then rises up a hill into a flat section opposite the station. The train is then launched backwards, through the loop and into the station.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Lime green track and dark green supports. The ride used to feature dark green track, it was repainted to its current scheme for the 2010 season.

fullText:
Sidewinder is a steel launched shuttle roller coaster located at Elitch Gardens in Denver, Colorado, USA. Built by Arrow Development, the ride opened in 1980 at Magic Springs. It is one of three Launched Loop installations still operating today.

The ride debuted in 1980 as Roaring Tornado at Magic Springs in Arkansas. It closed in 1989 and was sent to Elitch Gardens, where it reopened in 1990 as Sidewinder. Sidewinder was dismantled and rebuilt when Elitch Gardens moved to a new location for the 1995 season.

After standing but not operating since 2022, Sidewinder reopened on June 28, 2025. The ride's drive motor and control system were updated during its closure.

The train is launched down a hill and into a vertical loop. It then rises up a hill into a flat section opposite the station. The train is then launched backwards, through the loop and into the station.

Lime green track and dark green supports. The ride used to feature dark green track, it was repainted to its current scheme for the 2010 season.




 * Sidewinder on the park's website
